metadata.summary: pytest 8.x test suite authoring: fixtures, parametrization, markers, async testing, coverage, and CI integration.

content.title: Testing with pytest
content.summary: Covers pytest configuration, fixture scopes and composition, parametrize patterns, mocking with unittest.mock/pytest-mock, parallel execution with xdist, async testing, and coverage reporting. Primary testing framework for Python projects.

why.p: pytest is the de-facto Python test runner. Its fixture injection model, plugin ecosystem, and parametrize decorator eliminate boilerplate and enforce isolation. Knowing scope rules and conftest layering prevents the most common pytest anti-patterns that cause hard-to-debug test pollution.

list.item: Writing Python tests with pytest (unit, integration, or functional)
Designing fixture hierarchies (scope, composition, yield cleanup)
Setting up pytest configuration (pyproject.toml, markers, coverage)
Parametrizing tests to cover edge cases without duplication
Mocking external dependencies via unittest.mock or pytest-mock
Running tests in parallel with pytest-xdist
General fixture design patterns (framework-agnostic) — use test-fixtures

section.name: configuration
section.title: pytest Configuration
section.p: pyproject.toml setup, essential plugins, markers, coverage with pytest-cov, parallel execution with xdist, and asyncio mode.

rule.statement: Pin plugin versions in requirements-dev.txt or pyproject.toml dev dependencies.

example.code:
[tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths = ["tests"] python_files = ["test_*.py"] python_classes = ["Test*"] python_functions = ["test_*"] addopts = [ "--strict-markers", "--tb=short", "--cov=src", "--cov-report=term-missing", "--cov-report=html", "--cov-fail-under=80", ] asyncio_mode = "auto" markers = [ "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')", "integration: marks integration tests requiring external services", "e2e: marks end-to-end tests", ]
[tool.coverage.run] source = ["src"] omit = ["src/migrations/*", "src/settings/*"]
[tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines = [ "pragma: no cover", "if TYPE_CHECKING:", "raise NotImplementedError", ]

rule.statement: Declare all markers in pyproject.toml with --strict-markers to prevent typos.
Use markers to categorize slow or integration tests and skip them in fast feedback loops.

example.code:
# Fast unit tests only pytest -m "not slow and not integration"
# All including slow pytest
# Integration only pytest -m integration

rule.statement: Use pytest-xdist with -n auto for CPU-bound test suites; be aware of fixture scope issues.
Session-scoped fixtures are NOT shared across xdist workers — use module or function scope, or a tmp_path-based workaround.

antipattern.description: Session-scoped DB fixture breaks under xdist
antipattern.reason: Each worker gets its own session — database sequences can collide. Use separate DB per worker with worker_id fixture.

section.name: fixtures
section.title: pytest Fixtures
section.p: Fixture scopes (function/class/module/session), yield cleanup, conftest layering, factory fixtures, and autouse.

rule.statement: Default to function scope — it isolates tests and prevents state leakage.
Use session scope only for expensive, read-only resources (e.g., DB connection pool, loaded ML model).
A fixture can only depend on fixtures of equal or wider scope — never narrow scope from wide.

example.description: Session fixture depending on function-scoped fixture is invalid

rule.statement: Use yield fixtures instead of finalizers — the cleanup code is co-located with setup.

example.code: @pytest.fixture def tmp_file(tmp_path): path = tmp_path / "data.csv" path.write_text("col1,col2\n1,2\n") yield path # cleanup — runs even if test fails path.unlink(missing_ok=True)

rule.statement: Place shared fixtures in the nearest conftest.py — root conftest for global, subdir conftest for local.
Do not import conftest.py files directly — pytest discovers them automatically.

example.code: tests/ ├── conftest.py # db_session, settings, api_client ├── unit/ │ └── conftest.py # lightweight mocks only └── integration/ └── conftest.py # real DB fixtures

rule.statement: Use factory fixtures (functions returning factory functions) instead of returning pre-built objects.
Factory fixtures allow tests to customize the object while sharing setup code.
Use autouse=True sparingly — only for cross-cutting concerns like resetting global state or patching env vars.
Document autouse fixtures in conftest.py with a comment explaining why they are automatic.

section.name: patterns
section.title: pytest Test Patterns
section.p: AAA structure, parametrize (basic/IDs/cartesian/indirect), mocking with unittest.mock and pytest-mock, async tests, and debugging.

rule.statement: Every test follows Arrange → Act → Assert with a blank line between sections.
Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to test multiple cases without code duplication.
Always provide IDs for parametrized cases to make failure messages readable.
Use indirect parametrize to pass parameters through fixtures when fixture setup depends on the parameter.
Use mocker.patch() in test body (not conftest) for per-test mocks.
Use mocker.patch.object() when patching a method on a specific instance or class.
Always assert calls on mocks — silent mocks that never fire are useless.

antipattern.description: Patching at import location instead of usage location
antipattern.code:
# BAD: patches the original module, not where it's used mocker.patch("myapp.utils.requests.get")
# GOOD: patch where it's imported/used mocker.patch("myapp.services.payment.requests.get")
antipattern.reason: Python looks up names at call time in the namespace where they were imported, not the original module.

rule.statement: Set asyncio_mode = "auto" in pyproject.toml to avoid decorating every async test.
Use pytest-anyio or pytest-asyncio — never run event loops manually in tests.

example.code: # asyncio_mode = "auto" in pyproject.toml async def test_fetch_user(async_client): response = await async_client.get("/api/users/1") assert response.status_code == 200 assert response.json()["id"] == 1

template.purpose: pytest + coverage + xdist + asyncio config block
Base conftest.py with common fixtures (db, client, factory)
